1.5. We reiterated our recommendation and the Inventory Committee agreed to:
1.5.1. Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures for conducting the physical
count of PPE. This includes recognizing PPE items found at the station
and disposing of items that are non-existent or missing. The Accountant
and Property Officer should reconcile their records based on the results
of the actual physical count and make any necessary adjustments; and
1.5.2. Adopt a uniform numbering system for PPE property numbers and
require the Property Unit to update the property stickers according to
the prescribed format and include all the necessary information as
specified in Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020; and
1.5.3. Prepare the PIP for approval by the Municipal Mayor and submit the
same to COA at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of
inventory activities.

2.3.6. We recommended and Management agreed that the:
2.3.6.1. Inventory Committee conduct the physical count of the
Municipality’s local roads and thereafter, prepare and submit
the RPCLRN to the Audit Team, Municipal Accountant, and
Supply Officer;
2.3.6.2. Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the
Supply Officer with a complete description and segregation of
road components for road projects;
2.3.6.3. Municipal Accountant fully disclose the total road network
system in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
2.3.6.4. Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer, Municipal
Accountant, and Supply Officer to strictly comply with the
accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset
management system pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008
dated November 23, 2015.

3.4. We recommended and Management agreed that the:
3.4.1. Inventory Committee prepare and submit a copy of the inventory reports
to the Auditor to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and
3.4.2. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain the supplies
ledger cards and stock cards, respectively.

4.10. We recommended and the Municipal Agriculturist agreed to:
4.10.1. Conduct an inventory of breeding stocks as of the end of each year and
thereafter render a report including the fair market value of these items
to the Municipal Accountant, for proper recording in the books of the
LGU; and
4.10.2. Keep a systematic record of the Municipality’s breeding stocks,
reporting births, deaths, and disposal, and in case of death and disposal,
facilitate requests for relief of accountability in accordance with COA
Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, to appropriately drop the
amount from the books.

4.11. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose the
composition of the Biological Assets account in the Notes to Financial Statements
in accordance with IPSAS 27 to provide relevant information that may affect
the fairness of the presentation of the financial statements.

to the Audit Team the monthly BRS pursuant to Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

5.10. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer agreed to closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective
records for early detection and correction of adjustments and errors, if any,
pursuant to Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445.

002; and
6.9.2. Establish an efficient review and monitoring system to ensure that the
LDRRMFIP complies with the requirements outlined in COA Circular
No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) undertake cost-cutting measures and make appropriate adjustments to/reduction of the PS items in order to meet the PS limitation and avoid audit disallowances; b) strictly adhere to the foregoing provisions of LBC 98, LBC 108 and R.A. 7160 and desist from incurring additional PS costs; c) direct the immediate refund of the payment of non-mandatory PS cost increases in CY 2016, including the payment of PEI and implementation of the first tranche of the compensation adjustment pursuant to EO 201, series of 2016 which are subject to PS limitation; and d) require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the PS limitation in the preparation of the annual and supplemental budgets and to limit the release of allotments only to appropriations that have fully complied with the requirements of the law.

We recommended that the LDRRM and MPD Officers, fast-track the preparation of the CLUP and CPD, initiate the construction of the MRF and fully undertake measures to comply with the requirements to pass the SGLG and gain access to the PCF and the incentives package which could provide essential funding for the LGU’s programs in addressing the eventuality of a disaster.

We recommended that the Municipality identify, inventory and classify its insurable properties, submit the report to GSIS not later than October 31 of each year, appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General Fund or the 5% LDRRMF, and insure the same with the GIF under the GSIS every year.

We recommended that the BAC and TWG be properly supervised and be made accountable for lapses in the performance of their functions, as warranted. The BAC should indispensably prepare the checklists and require conformity in fulfilling all the requirements indicated therein in order to preserve the integrity of the bidding process as mandated under R.A. 9184.

We recommended that: a) The Municipal Mayor direct all offices to observe the cut-off period set by the Accounting Office for the submission of year-end obligations, closely monitor the LGU’s faithful compliance to the required submission of the accounts and reports, and institute the necessary disciplinary and justifiable actions for erring officials; b) The Municipal Accountant maintain a logbook or any other documentary record of the status of submission of accounts by the Municipal Treasurer, reduce into writing any follow-ups made with the latter for compliance to the aforementioned rules, set a cut-off date preferably before the end of the current fiscal year for the accommodation of unpaid obligations, and cause the preparation and submission of the accounts and reports within the reglementary period stipulated by law; and c) The Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer submit without undue delay the reports of collections, disbursements and checks issued to the Municipal Accountant in accordance with the timelines provided above.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Undertake cost-cutting measures and make appropriate adjustments to/reduction of the PS items in order to meet the PS limitation and avoid audit disallowances; b) Strictly adhere to the foregoing provisions of LBC 98, LBC 108 and R.A. 7160 and desist from incurring additional PS costs; c) Direct the immediate refund of the payment of non-mandatory PS cost increases in CY 2016, including the payment of PEI and implementation of the first tranche of the compensation adjustment pursuant to EO 201, series of 2016 which are subject to PS limitation; and d) Require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the PS limitation in the preparation of the annual and supplemental budgets and to limit the release of allotments only to appropriations that have fully complied with the requirements of the law.
observations.managementAction: Officials and employees refunded the productivity and incentives on installment basis and in some cases incentives deducted from terminal leave benefits.
observations.reason: Refunds were made on installment basis and in some cases deducted from terminal leave benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) Inventory Committee give priority to the 2013, 2014, 2011, 2012 and 2010 inventory guided by the regulations; b) Municipal Treasurer, as property custodian in the absence of a General Services Officer, secure all copies of the ARE and update the filing thereof; c) Municipal Accountant invariably require that the ARE be fully complied with, in form and quantity, as an indispensable supporting document for purchases of movable PPE; d) Management submit the schedule of PPE additions and disposals from CYs 2014-2016; and e) Municipal Accountant attach the formatted and detailed schedule of PPE additions and disposals to the yearly financial report of the LGU and regularly update it.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office gave priority to inventory but members of the committee could not agree on the timing of the physical count; Municipal Treasurer secured copies of the ARE where available and updated filing; Municipal Accountant required ARE compliance for purchases.
observations.reason: Physical count not fully completed; some AREs missing; reconciliation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LPRAT, through the BUB Focal Person, intensify efforts to fast-track the implementation of the BUB program for the LGU to achieve its target of reducing poverty and likewise avert any repercussions that may result from its low implementation rate by assigning technical personnel who will focus on BUB projects and by closely monitoring progress thereon. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all trust receipts, complete with details and reconciled with controlling accounts, in order to facilitate reporting on actual fund utilization.
observations.managementAction: The LGU implemented some actions; the BAC issued Notice to Proceed to the winning bidder on May 17, 2021.
observations.reason: Some projects in bidding stage; BAC issued Notice to Proceed for one project as of May 17, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management furnish our office the required documents that enumerated above and secure the 2012 application of title; pending compliance thereto, audit action on the pertinent transaction is hereby suspended.
observations.managementAction: The LGU submitted documents and is coordinating for the issuance of the Transfer Certificate of Title to the lot purchased as an indispensable requirement; pending compliance, the transaction was suspended.
observations.reason: Title has not yet been released; pending issuance of Transfer Certificate of Title by DENR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, spearheaded by the LDRRM and MPD Officers, fast-track the preparation of the CLUP and CDP, initiate the construction of the MRF and undertake measures to comply with the requirements to pass the SGLG and gain access to the PCF package which could provide essential funding for the LGU’s programs in addressing eventuality of a disaster.
observations.managementAction: The LGU collected data for the formulation of the CLUP and CDP; members of the technical working group for CLUP were scheduled to attend 6 modular trainings for the CLUP but were deferred because of the surge of COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: CLUP activities delayed due to COVID-19; trainings deferred.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality identify, inventory and classify its insurable properties, submit the report to the GSIS not later than October 31 of each year, in its annual budget under the General Fund or the 5% LDRRMF, and insure the same with the GIF under the GSIS every year.
observations.managementAction: Officials and employees insured some properties with the General Insurance Fund under GSIS where possible and furnished reports.
observations.reason: Except for the newly constructed public market, other properties were not insured due to absence of floor plans as basis of GSIS assessment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the salaries and other compensation paid to Mr. Rogelle Alve B. Serna from January 20, 2014 to October 9, 2014 under an appointment which has been disapproved by the Civil Service Commission be refunded to the municipal government by the person responsible for the unlawful appointment. We further recommend that Management furnish our office with a status report or pertinent documents, if already resolved, on the Motion for Reconsideration filed with the CSC for the appointment issued on October 30, 2014 which was disapproved by the CSCFO on April 7, 2015. Pending compliance thereto, audit action on payments made from October 30, 2014 to December 31, 2015 is hereby suspended.
observations.managementAction: Officials and employees refunded the amounts; the concerned employee is waiting for the decision of Regional Office No. XVI.
observations.reason: The concerned employee is awaiting CSC decision; full resolution pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC be properly supervised and be made accountable for lapses in the performance of their functions; the BAC should prepare the checklists and require conformity in fulfilling all the official requirements indicated therein in order to preserve the integrity of the bidding process as mandated under R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC prepared and submitted documents and an apology for the lapses of the BAC Secretariat in the conduct of the procurement as warranted.
observations.reason: The Audit Team is still reviewing the submitted documents; further actions pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inspectorate Team furnish our office with the operational and functional status of the project SALINTUBIG and the corresponding resources available to it to address the concerns on the functionality of the project and the corresponding actions taken and/or costs incurred, if any, to enable us to properly evaluate the transaction and make a decision in audit.
observations.managementAction: The LGU notified DOH Inspectorate Team to conduct inspection of the project; DOH conducted inspection and issued Certificate of Acceptance for SALINTUBIG project region VII. The LGU then submitted the operational and functional status report.
observations.reason: The DOH inspection and Certificate of Acceptance issued; final documentation submission pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the persons liable for the excess payment of the CY 2014 PEI enhancement immediately restitute the same to the government in accordance with Section 11.0 of DBM BC No. 2014-3. Henceforth, Management shall strictly observe the regulations on the grant of any benefits/incentives to avoid similar disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Officials and employees refunded the unauthorized productivity payments; management instructed strict observance of applicable regulations.
observations.reason: Refunds made; full compliance ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Mayor direct all offices to observe the cut-off period set by the accounting office for the submission of year-end obligations, closely monitor compliance to the required accounts and reports, and institute the necessary disciplinary actions for erring officials; b) The Municipal Accountant maintain a logbook or other documentary record of the status of submission of accounts by the Municipal Treasurer, reduce into writing any follow-ups made with the latter for compliance to the aforementioned rules, set a cut-off date preferably before the end of the current fiscal year for the accommodation of unpaid obligations, and cause the preparation and submission of the accounts and reports within the reglementary period stipulated by law; and c) The Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer submit without undue delay the reports of collections, disbursements and checks issued to the Municipal Accountant in accordance with the timelines provided above.
observations.managementAction: The officials and departments were instructed and an apology for lapses was submitted; the Accounting Office required quarterly trial balances; Municipal Accountant instituted monitoring and follow-ups.
observations.reason: Quarterly trial balances and reports submission improved but some delays remain; reconciliations and cut-off compliance remain in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the provisions of Section 37 of the General Appropriations Act of 2013 in the preparation of the payroll to ensure that employees’ net take home pay will not fall below P4,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adjusted payroll computations to ensure compliance with Section 37 and to avoid net take home pay falling below ₱4,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the amount due and collectible for the year with the certified list of taxpayers as required under Section 20 of the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer furnished the Municipal Accountant the amount due and collectible at the beginning of the year; the data were taken from the I-Tax program of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately update her cashbooks and henceforth, record her transactions in the cashbook daily pursuant to Sections 25, 26 and 27 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual, Volume II in order to facilitate the determination and verification of her accountability at any given time as well as the reconciliation of her cashbook with accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer updated the cashbooks and is recording transactions daily pursuant to Sections 25, 26 and 27 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Officer agreed to ensure that the amount required for GAD programs, projects and activities is appropriated in the annual budget and that only those projects which clearly address gender issues are appropriated as GAD projects and to ensure the attainment of GAD goals.
observations.managementAction: The Sanggunian Bayan passed a resolution appropriating the required amount.
observations.reason: Instead of decreasing the load allowances, the LGU increased the load allowance of the sangguniang bayan members, Vice Mayor from ₱1,000,00 and ₱2,000.00 to ₱2,000.00, and ₱3,000.00, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the salaries and other compensation paid to Mr. Rogelle Alve B. Serna from January 20, 2014 to October 9, 2014 under an appointment which has been disapproved by the Civil Service Commission with finality be refunded to the municipal government by the person responsible for the unlawful appointment. We further recommend that Management furnish our office with a status report or pertinent documents, if already resolved, on the Motion for Reconsideration filed with the CSC for the appointment issued on October 30, 2014 which was disapproved by the CSCFO on April 7, 2015. Pending compliance thereto, audit action on payments made from October 30, 2014 to December 31, 2015 is hereby suspended.
observations.managementAction: Mr. Rogelle Alve Serna filed an appeal to the Commission on Audit, Regional Office No. XVI.
observations.reason: The concerned employees are waiting for the decision of the Commission on Audit, Regional Office No. XVI.

observations.recommendation: a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in accordance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Real Property/Special Education Tax Receivables set-up at the beginning of the year was based on Municipal Treasurer’s estimate.
observations.reason: The Office of the Assessor did not furnish copy of the Real Property Assesment Roll to the Municipal Treasurer. The Real Property/Special Education Tax Receivables set-up at the beginning of the year was based on Municipal Treasurer’s estimate.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management, particularly the BAC Secretariat and the
Municipal Accountant, agreed to require all contractors to submit a Warranty
Security for Structural Defects and Failures as a supporting document to their
respective claims for final payment in accordance with Section 62.2.3.3 of the
Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer
prepare, at the beginning of each year, the certified list of taxpayers showing the
amount of real property taxes due and collectible for the year taken from the Real
Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card from the Municipal
Assessor’s Office as the basis of the Municipal Accountant to record the real
property tax and special education tax receivables as required under Section 20 of
the NGAS Manual, Volume I. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer
adopt a more aggressive tax collection policy to include posting in the municipal and
barangay halls a list of delinquent real property taxpayers, issuance of tax bills to be
sent out before December of each year, tapping the barangays for assistance in the
distribution thereof, and to consider recommending to the Mayor and the
Sanguniang Bayan the sale of delinquent properties through public auction.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct
periodic review and monitoring of the accounts payable account so that this
account will consist only of valid claims and to prepare the adjusting journal
entries to revert these to surplus, so that the Accounts Payable account will be
fairly presented in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the
properties in the books using the Certificates of Title and the respective tax
declarations as basis for the entries in order to fairly present the Land account in
the financial statements.

We recommended that the AAR and the Municipal Accountant desist from processing contractor’s claims unless these are duly supported by the documents required under P.D. 9184 and its IRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in order to ensure validity thereof. We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee see to it that the documents required under these law and regulations are complied in order to preserve the interest of the government. We also recommended that the Mayor consider formally terminating the contract and completing the project by administration in order that the structure can be utilized.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Bids and Awards Committee desist from processing contractor’s claims unless these are duly supported by the required documents. The Mayor terminated the contract.
observations.reason: The Mayor has not formally terminated the contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Inspector conducts a post validation to see to it that all conditions stated in the purchase orders are complied before accepting the delivery. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary correcting entries for the livestock purchased. Account was corrected in 2019 through Journal Entry Voucher together with the AOM.
observations.managementAction: The inspector conducts a post validation to see to it that all the conditions stated in the purchase orders are complied before accepting the delivery. The Municipal Accountant secured all copies of the AREs but did not conduct a physical count. Accounts were traced back and corrected in 2019 through Journal Entry Voucher together with the AOM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to submit the required documents so that the assets recorded in the trust fund can be totally charged from the books pursuant to Section 195 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments. We further recommended that the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards and property cards, respectively, in accordance with existing regulations. Lastly, we recommended the Municipality hire additional accounting personnel to augment the present manpower.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted the required documents to the Municipal Accountant for the dropping of the assets from the books; the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer enacted necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: It takes time to evaluate cost of the waste materials because the waste materials of the demolished public market was acquired long time ago.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality issue letters of demand to all officers who are still in the service and those who can still be contacted, after the filing of criminal charges on all who fail to liquidate, as provided under Section 9.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality issue letters of demand to all officers who are still in the service and those who can still be contacted, after the filing of criminal charges on all who fail to liquidate.
observations.reason: Although demand letters were not sent to the accountable officers, the LGU is still collecting the amount by withholding the unliquidated amounts from the terminal leave benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to implement most projects by contract instead of by administration considering the lack of technical personnel to supervise project implementation, or to hire additional engineering personnel to assist the Municipal Engineer in the implementation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The LGU implemented most projects by contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Mayor agreed to fill up the vacancies in the Local DRRM Office which should then be required to prepare plans and policies relative to the municipality’s preparedness for calamities and to initiate and monitor actions and measures which will strengthen the residents’ ability to respond to disasters. We further recommended the LGU to purchase early warning systems and preparedness equipment.
observations.managementAction: The LGU hired a LRDRRM Officer and implemented the following projects: Construction of two river control projects totaling ₱2,000,000.00, comfort rooms at the evacuation center costing ₱600,000.00, purchased canopies costing ₱150,000.00, and conducted CBMS profiling for ₱506,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor appoint or designate additional qualified personnel to the Office of the Social Welfare Officer so that the implementation of important social services such as those for the protection and welfare of the children can be attended to. We further recommended that the Local Council for the Protection of Children give importance to the more significant projects geared towards the attainment of the basic goals thereof.
observations.managementAction: The LGU hired one social worker on October 17, 2019.
observations.reason: Although, the LGU hired a social worker, the incumbent retired from government service in June 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant find all means to retrieve the records pertaining to the sources of the properties in CY 2012 and prior years in order to ascertain that assets recorded in the trust fund can be totally charged. Also, we recommended that the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards and property cards, respectively, in accordance with existing regulations. Lastly, we recommended the Municipality hire additional accounting personnel to augment the present manpower.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal accounting personnel are tracing back transactions to ascertain available documents and to retrieve records.
observations.reason: Limited time/personnel hindered the LGU from retrieving available documents to ascertain records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality hire additional accounting personnel to augment the current manpower of the Municipal Accounting Office. Meantime, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements within ten (10) days from receipt after the end of each month as provided under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 so that the validity and existence of the cash in bank could be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office prepared reconciliation statements up to December 31, 2018 and identified reconciling items which remain unadjusted.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan institute internal rules to regulate, reduce and limit the grant of load allowance to its officials who should at least be a Chief of Division pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003. We further recommended subscription to mobile phone plans and offering unlimited talk and text for very low prices in order to preclude the excessive mobile phone expenses.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan passed a resolution prescribing amounts for mobile phone load allowances.
observations.reason: Instead of decreasing the load allowances, the LGU increased the load allowance of the sangguniang bayan members, Vice Mayor from ₱1,000,00 and ₱2,000.00 to ₱2,000.00, and ₱3,000.00, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the related transactions, locate erroneous journal entries and prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reconcile the Due To/From Other Funds accounts in the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund books as required in Sections 28 and 152 of the NGAS Manual, Volume III. We further recommended that the accounting unit regularly reconcile these two reciprocal accounts to ensure the fairness of presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant located necessary supporting documents and adjusted the accounts in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: There are some accounts which were not adjusted accordingly due to incomplete records kept by the previous accounting personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Budget Officer appropriate at least one percent (1%) of the municipality’s IRA for the LCPC and utilize the same for programs, projects and activities that would safeguard and protect the welfare of the children in accordance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer appropriated at least one percent (1%) of the municipality’s IRA for the LCPC in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that claims are duly supported with all the required supporting documents in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in order to ensure the validity, propriety and legality thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured disbursement vouchers were supported with complete documentation as required.

observations.recommendation: Management: a) Undertake cost-cutting measures and make appropriate adjustments to/reduction of the PS items in order to meet the PS limitation and avoid audit disallowances; b) Strictly adhere to the provisions of LBC 98, LBC 108 and R.A. 7160 and desist from incurring additional PS costs; c) Direct the immediate refund of the payment of non-mandatory PS cost increases in CY 2016, including the payment of PEI and implementation of the first tranche of the compensation adjustment pursuant to EO 201, series of 2016 which are subject to PS limitation; and d) Require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the PS limitation in the preparation of the annual and supplemental budgets and to limit the release of allotments only to appropriations that have fully complied with the requirements of the law.
observations.managementAction: Officials and employees refunded the amounts via installment basis productivity and enhancement incentive; in some cases deducted from the terminal leave benefits.
observations.reason: The refund was done on installment basis and in some cases deducted from the terminal leave benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a) Inventory Committee give priority to the completion of the physical count guided by the regulations and reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records; b) Municipal Treasurer, as property custodian in the absence of a General Services Officer, secure all copies of the ARE and update the filing thereof, ensuring that these are principally considered especially during the separation of accountable officers from the government service; c) Municipal Accountant invariably require that the ARE be fully complied with, in form and quantity, as an indispensable supporting document for purchases of movable PPE; d) Management submit the schedule of PPE additions and disposals from CYs 2014-2016, complete with data on the property tag, ARE, accountable officer, office/location of the equipment, and the actual DVs and ARE; otherwise, those that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed; and e) Municipal Accountant attach the formatted and detailed schedule of PPE additions and disposals to the yearly financial report of the LGU and regularly update it to facilitate the conduct of the physical inventory and to pinpoint responsibility for government properties.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer secured all copies of the AREs but did not conduct the physical count.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and knowledge as well as the absence of office personnel hindered the LGU from conducting the physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LPRAT, through the BUB Focal Person, intensify the efforts to fast-track the implementation of the BUB program for the LGU to achieve its target of reducing poverty and likewise avert any repercussions that may result from its low implementation rate by assigning technical personnel who will focus on BUB projects and by closely monitoring progress thereon. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all trust receipts, complete with details and reconciled with controlling accounts, in order to facilitate reporting on actual fund utilization.
observations.managementAction: The LGU implemented various projects and the accounting office maintained records.
observations.reason: The construction of health station was deferred because the site intended for the health station has not been donated.

observations.recommendation: Management submit the required documents of title and secure the Transfer Certificate of Title to the lot purchased as an indispensable requirement.
observations.managementAction: The documents for application of title were re-submitted to DENR for evaluation.
observations.reason: Title has not yet been released. Pending compliance thereto, audit action on the pertinent transaction is hereby suspended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, spearheaded by the LDRRM and MPD Officers, fast-track the preparation of the CLUP and CDP, initiate the construction of the MRF and fully undertake measures to comply with the requirements to pass the SGLG and gain access to the PCF and the incentives package which could provide essential funding for the LGU’s programs in addressing the eventuality of a disaster.
observations.managementAction: The data for the Comprehensive Land Use Plan preparation was gathered and the CLUP preparation covers six modules.
observations.reason: LGU officials did not attend the six months training for CLUP modules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipality identify, inventory and classify its insurable properties, submit the report to the GSIS not later than October 31 of each year, appropriate necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General Fund or the 5% LDRRMF, and insure the same with the GSIS every year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal allocated ₱400,000.00 for insurance but property was not insured because of the absence of a floor plan as basis of GSIS in assessing the insurable value.
observations.reason: The property was not insured because of the absence of a floor plan as basis of GSIS in assessing the insurable value.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC and TWG be properly supervised and be made accountable for lapses in the performance of their functions, as warranted. The BAC should indispensably prepare the checklists and require conformity in fulfilling all the official requirements indicated therein in order to preserve the integrity of the procurement process as mandated under R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: All lacking documents mentioned were submitted to COA, together with an apology for the lapses of the BAC and the BAC Secretariat in the conduct of the prescribed procurement process.
observations.reason: The audit team is still in the process of reviewing the documents submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the persons liable for the excess payment of the CY 2014 immediately restitute the same to the government, in accordance with Section 11.0 of DBM BC No. 2014-3. Henceforth, Management shall strictly observe the applicable regulations on issuance of Certificate of Project Completion and Certification of Project Acceptance. We further recommended that the LGU Inspectorate Team furnish our office with the Certification of Project Completion and Certification of Project Acceptance issued by the DOH to the LGU; submit an operational and functional status report of the project, the recourses available to it to address the concerns on the functionality of the project and the corresponding actions taken and/or costs incurred, if any, to enable us to properly evaluate the transaction and make a decision in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Inspectorate Team requested DOH Regional Office for issuance of Certificate of Project Completion and Certification of Project Acceptance; the LGU submitted operational certification of the project as acceptance from Department of Health Region VII is pending.
observations.reason: To date, not all the required DOH documents were re-submitted. Title has not yet been released.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the persons liable for the excess payment of the CY 2014 PEI immediately restitute the same to the government, in accordance with Section 11.0 of DBM BC No. 2014-3. Henceforth, Management shall strictly observe the applicable regulations on the grant of any benefits/incentives to avoid similar disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management instructed all offices to observe cut-off period and to submit necessary reports; some officials and employees were able to refund disallowed amounts.
observations.reason: To date, not all officials and employees were able to refund their excess PEI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Mayor direct all offices to observe the cut-off period set by the Accounting Office for the submission of year-end obligations, closely monitor the LGU’s faithful compliance to the required submission of the accounts and reports and institute the necessary disciplinary and justifiable actions for erring officials; b) The Municipal Accountant maintain a logbook or any other documentary record of the status of submission of accounts by the Municipal Treasurer, Report of Checks Issued and reduce into writing any follow-ups made with the latter for compliance to the aforementioned rules; and c) The Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer submit without undue delay the reports of collections, disbursements and checks issued to the Municipal Accountant in accordance with the timelines provided above.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed all offices to observe the cut-off period and the Accounting Office will stamp on the Reports of Checks Issued the actual date of receipt of the vouchers to establish compliance with the reglementary period for submission.
observations.reason: The accounting office failed to submit financial reports on or before February 28, 2020 due to the lack of accounting manpower. In addition to the logbook, the accounting office will stamp on the report of Checks Issued the actual date of receipt of the vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor set a time frame for the MSWMB to formulate the Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan forthwith considering the length of time that has lapsed since the effectivity of R.A. 9003, to which the Municipality has not complied, and to avoid possible administrative sanctions embodied in Section 50 of the Act.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board formulated the Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) Observe the provisions of Section 37 of the General Appropriations Act of 2013 in the preparation of the payroll to ensure that employees’ net take home pay will not fall below P4,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Renewal of loans for those with net take home pay below ₱4,000.00 were done before the audit observation was issued.
observations.reason: Some employees had already renewed their loans for those below ₱4,000.00 before the audit observation was issued.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately update her cashbooks and to henceforth, record her transactions in the cashbook daily pursuant to Sections 25, 26 and 27 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual, Volume II in order to facilitate the determination and verification of her accountability at any given time as well as the reconciliation of her cashbook with accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Posting of transactions are done daily by the Municipal Treasurers Office. The accounting office will reduce into writing any follow-ups made with the latter for compliance to the reglementary period of submission.
observations.reason: Although posting of transactions are done daily by the Municipal Treasurers Office, reconciliation between the cashbooks and journals are not done regularly because of the lack of manpower.

observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: The LGU did not give importance to this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality identify, inventory and classify its insurable properties, submit the report to the GSIS not later than October 31 of each year, appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General Fund or the 5% LDRRMF, and insure the same with the GIF under the GSIS every year.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: The LGU did not give importance to this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant find all means
to retrieve the records pertaining to the sources of the properties in CY 2012 and
prior years in order that assets recorded in the trust fund can be totally transferred
to the proper accounts in the general fund for proper presentation of the financial
statements pursuant to Section 104 of the Manual on NGAS. Also, we
recommended that the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer
maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards and property cards,
respectively, in accordance with existing regulations. Lastly, we recommended that
the Municipality hire additional accounting personnel to augment the present
manpower.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional accounting personnel
to augment the current manpower of the Municipal Accounting Office. Meantime,
require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation
statements within ten (10) days from receipt after the end of each month as provided
under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 so that the
validity and existence of the cash in bank could be ascertained.

We recommended that in the preparation of the Report of Checks Issued, the
Municipal Treasurer include only those checks which have already been released to
claimants in order to accurately present the amount of disbursements. In addition,
we recommend that the Municipal Accountant take up an adjusting entry for the
unreleased checks which were taken up in the books.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan institute internal rules to
regulate, reduce and limit the grant of load allowance to its officials who should at
least be a Chief of Division pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003. We further
recommended subscription to mobile phone plans offering unlimited talk and text
for very low prices in order to preclude the incurrence of excessive mobile phone
expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist constantly monitor the
implementation of the cattle raising livelihood project in order to ensure its success
and sustainability. We also recommended that the contract be amended to include
the important conditions and for the LGU to impose the sanctions in case of
contract violations in order that the project can continue.

We recommended and management agreed that in the preparation of
Programs of Work, the Municipal Engineer first conduct site investigation/survey in
order that these will be more accurately prepared and thus protect the interest of
the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the related
transactions, locate the erroneous journal entries and prepare the necessary
adjusting entry to reconcile the Due To/From Other Funds accounts in the General
Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund books as required in Sections 28 and
152 of the NGAS Manual, Volume III. We further recommended that the
accounting unit regularly reconcile these two reciprocal accounts to ensure the
fairness of presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer’s Office (MEO) ensure that
newly-cemented roads are closed to traffic while in the curing phase, and to also
regularly monitor the project to see whether the barriers are still in place and not
moved by motorists. We further recommended that the MEO conduct immediate
repair on the cracks and scaling in order to avoid further deterioration.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Budget Officer
appropriate at least one percent (1%) of the municipality’s IRA for the LCPC and
utilize the same for programs, projects and activities that would safeguard and
protect the welfare of the children in accordance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344
and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended that teacher-aides, nurses and midwives concerned be
required to prepare and submit duly accomplished Daily Time Records (DTRs)
showing the actual time of arrival at and departure from station to support the
payrolls for salaries and wages as required by existing regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that claims are duly
supported with all the required supporting documents in accordance with Section
4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in order to ensure
the validity, propriety and legality thereof.

We recommended that Management: a) Undertake cost-cutting measures and make appropriate adjustments to/reduction of the PS items in order to meet the PS limitation and avoid audit disallowances; b) Strictly adhere to the foregoing provisions of LBC 98, LBC 108 and R.A. 7160 and desist from incurring additional PS costs; c) Direct the immediate refund of the payment of non-mandatory PS cost increases in CY 2017, including the payment of PEI and implementation of the first tranche of the compensation adjustment pursuant to EO 201, series of 2017 which are subject to PS limitation; and d) Require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the PS limitation in the preparation of the annual and supplemental budgets and to limit the release of allotments only to appropriations that have fully complied with the requirements of the law.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 budget implemented. Vacant positions were not filled-up.
observations.reason: Management promised that it will be fully implemented in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify the PIs recorded in the various registries that would compose the 25% requirement, prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to facilitate recognition thereof in the books and, henceforth, ensure that the required rate of PI recognition is complied with every year-end until all the PIs are recorded back in the books by the end of CY 2019.
observations.managementAction: 2016 Registry of Public Infrastructure were transferred in the Equity Account per unnumbered JEV dated December 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a) Inventory Committee give priority to the completion of the physical count guided by the 2014 regulations prescribed under Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I, reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records, prepare the report for submission to our office and pinpoint responsibility for missing properties; b) Municipal Treasurer, as property custodian in the absence of a General Services Officer, secure all copies of the ARE and update the filing thereof, ensuring that these are principally considered especially during the separation of accountable officers from the government service; c) Municipal Accountant invariably require that the ARE be fully complied with, in form and quantity, as an indispensable supporting document for purchases of movable PPE; d) Management submit the schedule of PPE additions and disposals from CYs 2014-2017, complete with data on the property tag, ARE, accountable officer, office/location of the equipment, and the actual DVs and ARE; otherwise, those that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed; and e) Municipal Accountant attach the formatted and detailed schedule of PPE additions and disposals to the yearly financial report of the LGU and regularly update it to facilitate the conduct of the physical inventory and to pinpoint responsibility for government properties.
observations.managementAction: ARE were attached to 2013 List and Schedule of PPE were attached to the 2012 year-end financial statement.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee did not submit the report of physical inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LPRAT, through the BUB Focal Person, intensify efforts to fast-track the implementation of the BUB program for the LGU to achieve its target of reducing poverty and likewise avert any repercussions that may result from its low implementation rate by assigning technical personnel who will focus on BUB projects and by closely monitoring progress thereon. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all trust receipts, complete with details and reconciled with controlling accounts, in order to facilitate reporting on actual fund utilization.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted a status report as of June 2017 showing program of work and improvement in the number of projects implemented under BUB.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to assist the processing of program of work and detailed engineering design hinder the completion of all projects in BUB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the required documents enumerated above and secure the Transfer Certificate of Title to the lot purchased as an indispensable requirement. Pending compliance thereto, audit action on the pertinent transaction is hereby suspended.
observations.managementAction: Already applied for land titling and awaiting the issuance of title.
observations.reason: Copy of title not yet released.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a) Municipal Treasurer ensure complete submission of accounts within the period required by law so as not to impede the preparation of the BRS and request directly from the bank for copies of bank statements which could no longer be retrieved; b) Municipal Accountant assign staff to secure the monthly bank statements either from the Treasurer’s Office or directly from the bank and fast-track the preparation of the BRS in order to effect timely adjustments to the recorded balances and to pinpoint accountability for any irregular bank items or unauthorized bank transactions; c) Municipal Mayor closely monitor the regular submission of the BRS to the Auditor for verification and ensure settlement of accountabilities for any unauthorized reconciling items so determined; and d) Management accord utmost importance to this critical and indispensable tool in order to minimize the risk of loss/misuse of depository funds and to avoid probable sanctions for gross negligence in the performance of duties.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was done until April 2017.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower the reconciliation was not fully done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Require the former Municipal Treasurer to update her accounts and establish and settle her accountability as of retirement date to facilitate the turnover to the Acting Municipal Treasurer as a requirement for the issuance of her clearance; b) Direct the former Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to account for the differences noted between the ledger and cashbook balances, and henceforth conduct a quarterly reconciliation or as often as necessary; and c) Closely monitor the Acting Municipal Treasurer’s compliance to the strict requirements pertaining to the handling and custody of cash and depository accounts.
observations.managementAction: Accounting record is in place and ready for matching with cashbook.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Treasurer did not fully update her cash book.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor activate the LCPC to ensure its functionality and give priority to the implementation of the PPAs for the fund has been allocated and the formulation of the LDPC, LIPC and the LSCR. We further recommended that the LCPC coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in ensuring that the plans and budget are adequately funded, utilized and reported on through the Accomplishment Report so as to provide monitoring measures for the attainment of the program’s objectives.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Finding No. 9 of the report.
observations.reason: Although the LGU appropriated and utilized the LCPC amount was below the required 1% of IRA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer facilitate the immediate release of the remaining SEF shares and ensure that all collections for special education tax, especially those collected by the Provincial Treasurer’s Office and acknowledged thru the issuance of AF 51, are properly included in the computation of the correct shares. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the actual status of Check No. 42126332 and should it be determined that the check cleared the bank, hold the former Municipal Treasurer accountable for it, otherwise, as a stale check, coordinate with the acting Municipal Treasurer to replace the lost check.
observations.managementAction: Collections were already transferred to SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the salaries and other compensation paid to Mr. Rogelle Alve B. Serna from January 20, 2014 to October 9, 2014 under an appointment which has been disapproved by the Civil Service Commission with finality be refunded to the municipal government by the person responsible for the unlawful appointment. We further recommend that Management furnish our office with a status report or pertinent documents, if already resolved, on the Motion for Reconsideration filed with the CSC for the appointment issued on October 30, 2014 which was disapproved by the CSCFO on April 7, 2015. Pending compliance thereto, audit action on payments made from October 30, 2014 to December 31, 2015 is hereby suspended.
observations.managementAction: Appeal was made to COA - NIR within the prescribed period provided under the New COA Rules of Procedure.
observations.reason: Awaiting final resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management review the existing DRRM projects and programs outlined under the 70% MF-MOOE to determine those which may no longer be feasible so that the allocated funds could be included in the CY 2017 LDRRMF Investment Plan and reprogrammed for more relevant activities aligned with the NDRRM Plan 2011-2028. For the 70% MF-CO, we further recommended that Management prioritize its evaluation as planned to ensure the availability of adequate facilities/skills in the event of disasters.
observations.managementAction: Conducted a 2-day LDRRMC planning workshop assisted by DILG personnel on August 18 and 19, 2017. Conducted council meeting for the inclusion in the AIP and reappropriation.
observations.reason: Some other items of appropriations are no longer allowed under 2013 and need council resolution for reappropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: a) Management replenish the 5% LDRRMF from the General Fund Proper through a duly authorized appropriation ordinance passed by the Sangguniang Bayan and furnish our office with the said document, together with the original Journal Entry Voucher taking up the reappropriation; otherwise, we shall be constrained to disallow the same in audit; b) The MDRRMC reappropriate the amount of P530,660.00 for projects and activities compliant with the Joint NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-CSC MC No. 2014-1 and Joint NDRRMC-DBM-DILG MC No. 2013-1; and c) The Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the LDRRMF Investment Plan passed by the MDRRMC thru the Local Development Council as adapted in the Annual Investment Plan is compliant with the pertinent regulations to avoid inappropriate uses of the said fund.
observations.managementAction: The amount was already replenished from the General Fund. Amount was added back to the account for reappropriation in the LDRRMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC and TWG be properly supervised and be made accountable for lapses in the performance of their functions, as warranted. The BAC should indispensably prepare the checklists and require conformity in fulfilling all the official requirements indicated therein in order to preserve the integrity of the bidding process as mandated under R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Settlement with regards to AOM 2017-09 dated 3-25-16 and NS 16-01-100 dated June 7, 2017 was made on June 23, 2017. All lacking documents mentioned were submitted to COA, together with the apology in the lapse of BAC and BAC Secretariat of not observing the prescribed process of procurement law. Religiously observed the provisions of RA 9184.
observations.reason: For final resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exhaust all means to implement without further delay the projects funded out of the 20% DF and fast track the backlogs in prior years in order to minimize idle funds and maximize the usefulness of such projects. Any favorable action given hereto will ultimately redound to the benefit of the Municipality and its constituents, and to the achievement of the overall objective of R.A. 7160 to make LGUs self-reliant communities.
observations.managementAction: Some projects are already started, others are awarded and the management decided to hire skilled person to prepare DED and POW to augment the Engineering work force.
observations.reason: Lack of Technical personnel to assist in the implementation of projects. LGU lacks Heavy Equipment to be used and the existing equipment cannot accommodate the implementation of all the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the persons liable for the excess payment of the CY 2014 PEI immediately restitute the same to the government, in accordance with Section 11.0 of DBM BC No. 2014-3. Henceforth, Management shall strictly observe the applicable regulations on the grant of any benefits/incentives to avoid similar disallowed disbursements. (Note: original recommendation text tied to SALINTUBIG also asked for the LGU Inspectorate Team to furnish documentation and certification to assist audit evaluation.)
observations.managementAction: Management was able to collect payment of refund from some municipal officials and employees for the settlement of the disallowed amount. Constant reminders were verbally made.
observations.reason: For final resolution. As to project operational status, cannot ascertain stability of the water sources in CY 2017 because it eventually vanished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the persons liable for the excess payment of the CY 2014 PEI immediately restitute the same to the government, in accordance with Section 11.0 of DBM BC No. 2014-3. Henceforth, Management shall strictly observe the applicable regulations on the grant of any benefits/incentives to avoid similar disallowed disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to collect payment of refund from some municipal officials and employees for the settlement of the disallowed amount. Constant reminders were verbally made.
observations.reason: Not all officials and employees have refunded their excess PEI received in 2014.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Mayor direct all offices to observe the cut-off period set by the Accounting Office for the submission of year-end obligations, closely monitor the LGU’s faithful compliance to the required submission of the accounts and reports, and institute the necessary disciplinary and justifiable actions for erring officials; b) The Municipal Accountant maintain a logbook or any other documentary record of the status of submission of accounts by the Municipal Treasurer, reduce into writing any follow-ups made with the latter for compliance to the aforementioned rules, set a cut-off date preferably before the end of the current fiscal year for the accommodation of unpaid obligations, and cause the preparation and submission of the accounts and reports within the reglementary period stipulated by law; and c) The Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer submit without undue delay the reports of collections, disbursements and checks issued to the Municipal Accountant in accordance with the timelines provided above.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. Kept reminding the Partially Disbursing Officer. Treasurer still delayed in the submission of accounts.
observations.reason: Treasurer still delayed in the submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the MSWMB to formulate the Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan in order to maximize the benefits forthwith considering the existing length of time that has lapsed since the effectivity of R.A. 9003, to which the Municipality has not complied, and to avoid possible administrative sanctions embodied in Section 50 of the Act.
observations.managementAction: Executive Order has been made for the reactivation of the SWMB.
observations.reason: Still waiting for the approval of the said plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to all incumbent and previous personnel for the liquidation of their outstanding cash advances, citing the sanctions which will be imposed on them for failure to liquidate, such as withholding of salaries and other emoluments due them, the initiation of administrative proceedings and the filing of malversation charges under Articles 217 and 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters have been served to the concerned officials and employees. Existing cash advances substantially reduced after the service of demand letters.
observations.reason: Municipal officials and employees’ cash advances remained unliquidated despite the demand. However, additional cash advances were granted to those who have existing unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor: a) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to transfer the book value of unserviceable properties to the Other Assets account; and b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to dispose of unserviceable properties in accordance with disposal provisions of COA Circular 92-386 so that the municipal government can still realize additional income therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation and verification of physical inventory report was made against accounting record for proper identification of assets to be transferred and disposed.
observations.reason: Inventory report available is only partial and lacks details necessary for the disposal of property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Observe the provisions of Section 37 of the General Appropriations Act of 2013 in the preparation of the payroll to ensure that employees’ net take home pay will not fall below P4,000.00; and b) The Municipal Treasurer should stop amortization/loan deductions where applicable.
observations.managementAction: Partial restitution was made. Kept reminding the Partially Disbursing Officer. Some deductions could not be stopped automatically due to existing MOA/contractual agreements entered into by the municipality. We are effecting deductions until fully paid.
observations.reason: Some employee deductions cannot be stopped automatically due to existing MOA/contractual agreement. We are still effecting the deductions until fully paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to hasten the preparation of Programs of Work and Plans for the projects to be implemented in order that the 20% Development Fund can be fully utilized and the target projects implemented in time.
observations.managementAction: Assessor conducted general revision, with program ready for implementation. Some DED are prepared and ready for implementation. Conducted a planning workshop. Project implementation is on-going.
observations.reason: LGU lacks Heavy Equipment to be used and the existing equipment cannot accommodate the implementation of all the projects. Only one person performing the job; lack of personnel to do the work.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in accordance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Assessor conducted general revision, with Tax program ready for implementation.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to do the work. Only one person performing the job.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommend that the municipal treasurer should immediately restitute the funds of P384,325.31 initially found missing including others, if any, that may still be discovered upon final determination of her accountability. We also reiterate the recommendation that the municipal treasurer should be immediately relieved from her duties as such. The local chief executive should also institute appropriate action for her dereliction of duties and malversation of public funds. Henceforth, the local chief executive should strictly require all accountable officers whose duties permit or require the custody of government funds to refrain from using these funds to accommodate vales from fellow officials and employees, otherwise, they should be dealt with accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Partial restitution was made. The municipality appointed an Acting Municipal Treasurer. Municipal Treasurer already retired from service. Municipal Accountant appointed.
observations.reason: This amount is part of the total shortage of Municipal Treasurer already retired from service. The balance of ₱577,787.38 as per Municipal Accountant’s certification dated April 6, 2018. The amount returned out of the total shortage of ₱384,325.31 as stated in this report cannot be determined.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately update her cashbooks and to henceforth, record her transactions in the cashbook daily pursuant to Sections 25, 26 and 27 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual, Volume II in order to facilitate the determination and to submit her accountability at any given time as well as the reconciliation of her cashbook with accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Posted transactions to cashbook every Friday. Retired ex-Treasurer is mandated and required to update her cashbook. Accounting record is in place and updated.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous work, transactions were not posted to the cash book on a daily basis. The retired ex-Treasurer did not post all transactions daily; updated records were later submitted.

observations.managementAction: Appeal was made to COA - NIR within the prescribed period provided under the New COA Rules and Procedures.
observations.reason: Awaiting final resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan, upon recommendation of the Municipal Mayor, enact an ordinance institutionalizing the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and appropriate adequate funds necessary for its operation, including the staffing requirements in compliance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1.
observations.managementAction: Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and Positions had been created and LDRRM Officer was appointed last December 2015.
