observations.reason: Appropriations are no longer allowed under JMC 2013 and need council resolution for reappropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher transferring the amount of P3.12million from the General Fund to the Trust Fund using the accounts discussed in the foregoing paragraphs in compliance with the accounting requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry Voucher has been prepared, dated June 27, 2016 and funds transferred to Trust Fund. Copy of the JEV furnished to the auditor during the exit conference.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management replenish the 5% LDRRMF from the General Fund through a duly authorized appropriation ordinance passed by Sangguniang Bayan and furnish our office with the said document, together with the Journal Entry Voucher taking up the adjustments; otherwise, we shall be constrained to disallow the same in audit; b) The MDRRMC reappropriate the amount of P530,660.00 for projects and activities compliant with the Joint NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-CSC MC No. 2014-1 and Joint NDRRMC-DBM-DILG MC No. 2013-1; and c) The Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the LDRRMF Investment Plan passed by the MDRRMC thru the Local Development Council as adapted in the Annual Investment Plan is compliant with the pertinent regulations to avoid inappropriate uses of the said fund.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMF replenishment is included in the 2017 Annual Budget.
observations.reason: The amount needed to replenish the fund was not included in the 2017 Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management discontinue deducting from the payroll any payment to the aforementioned private financing institutions, except for loans availed by the employees prior to receipt of this Audit Observation Memorandum; henceforth, desist from entering into similar contracts with financing institutions not otherwise authorized under the GAA.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped issuing net take home fee certification for loan remittances.
observations.reason: Continued the deductions of loan granted prior to the observation. We are effecting deductions until fully paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management stop immediately the payment of the employer’s share to the Pag-IBIG Fund in excess of P100.00 and require employee-beneficiaries, including all officials who authorized such excessive contributions, to refund the same to the government contribution to P 100.00 pesos only effective May 2016 remittances.
observations.managementAction: Management discontinued and adjusted the payment of employer’s share remittances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC and TWG be properly supervised and be made accountable for lapses in the performance of their functions, as warranted. The BAC should indispensably prepare the checklists and require conformity in fulfilling all the requirements indicated therein in order to preserve the integrity of the bidding process as mandated under R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Settlement with regards to AOM 2016-09 dated 3-25-16 and NS 16-01-100 dated June 7, 2016 was made on June 23, 2016. All lacking documents mentioned were submitted to COA, together with the apology in the lapse of BAC and BAC Secretariat of not observing the prescribed process of procurement law. Religiously observed the provisions of RA 9184.
observations.reason: For final resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management exhaust all means to implement without further delay the projects and backlogs in prior years in order to minimize idle funds and maximize the usefulness of such projects. Any favorable action given hereto will ultimately redound to the benefit of the Municipality and its constituents, and to the achievement of the overall objective of R.A. 7160 to make LGUs self-reliant communities.
observations.managementAction: Some projects are already started, others are awarded and management decided to hire skilled person to prepare DED and POW and augment Engineering work force.
observations.reason: Lack of Technical personnel to assist in the implementation of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant fast-track the preparation of the PPSAS-compliant CY 2015 financial statements and submit them to our office at the soonest possible time considering these are long overdue.
observations.managementAction: PPSAS-compliant financial statement for CY 2015 was submitted to COA together with the necessary supporting schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU Inspectorate Team furnish our office with the Certificate of Project Completion and Certification of Project Acceptance issued by the DOH to the LGU. We further recommended that the LGU Inspectorate Team submit an operational and functional status of the project, the available recourses to address the concerns on the functionality of the project and the corresponding actions taken and/or costs incurred, if any, to enable us to properly evaluate the transaction and make a decision in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Team conducted site inspection and found out that the water source abundant can no longer be found for reasons beyond human comprehension. Certification from DOH R-7 after they conducted an onsite inspection. As to project operational status, we cannot ascertain the stability of the water sources because it kept changing its course and eventually vanished.
observations.reason: Some other items of project operational status cannot be ascertained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a) Instruct the new Inventory Committee to plan and strategize the conduct of the physical count of properties every year; b) Submit the schedule of PPE additions and disposals within the year 2014, complete with the property tag, Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment, accountable officer, office/location of the equipment; otherwise, those that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed; and c) For unjustified failure to submit the inventory report, implement the sanction provided in Sections 122 and 127 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: New Inventory Committee was created. No available training for inventory committee in the conduct of actual inventory and un-availability of ARE from the issued ARE from the equipment/property.
observations.reason: Members of the Inventory Committee not property equipped with the knowledge in the conduct of physical inventory and un-availability of ARE from the issued equipment/property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the persons liable for the excess payment of the CY 2014 PEI immediately restitute the same to the government.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to collect payment of refunded amounts from some municipal officials and employees for the settlement of the disallowed amount. Constant reminder was made.
observations.reason: Not all officials and employees have refunded their excess PEI received in 2014.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in accordance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; b) The Municipal Accountant maintain a logbook or any other documentary record of the status of submission of accounts by the Municipal Treasurer, reduce into writing any follow-ups made with the latter for compliance to the aforementioned rules, set a cut-off date preferably before the end of the current fiscal year for the accommodation of unpaid obligations, and cause the preparation and submission of the accounts and reports within the reglementary period stipulated by law; c) The Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer submit without undue delay the reports of collections, disbursements and checks issued to the Municipal Accountant in accordance with the timelines provided above.
observations.managementAction: Maintained record to monitor the submission of accounts, and made follow-ups regarding the status of submission. Kept reminding the Disbursing Officer and the Treasurer on the status of submission. The treasurer submitted his report of disbursement on time, but the Treasurer was still delayed in the submission of accounts.
observations.reason: Treasurer still delayed in the submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Set a time frame for the MSWMB to formulate the Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan in forthwith considering the length of time that has lapsed since the effectivity of R.A. 9003, to which the Municipality has not complied, and to avoid possible administrative sanctions embodied in Section 50 of the Act.
observations.managementAction: Plans and Budget are ready for the construction of storage facilities such as Sanitary Landfill, Central MRF and RCA. The Ten-Year integrated Solid Waste Management Plan was submitted last year 2015 to the DENR-EMB Regional and National offices.
observations.reason: Still waiting for the approval of the said plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site; b) Concretize methods and procedures for the phaseout and the eventual closure of existing open and/or controlled dumps within a specific time frame; and c) Close/open dumps and secure with perimeter fence.
observations.managementAction: Open Dumpsite officially closed and secured with perimeter fence.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the meantime that a cash examination cannot be conducted, the Mayor sternly require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections in her hands within three days from receipt of this memorandum.
observations.managementAction: The undeposited 2010 collection was deposited during the first week of January 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to all incumbent and previous personnel for the liquidation of their outstanding cash advances, citing the sanctions which will be imposed on them for failure to liquidate, such as withholding of salaries and other emoluments due them, the initiation of administrative proceedings and the filing of malversation charges under Articles 217 and 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: Demand letter had been served to the concerned AAR officials and employees. Partial restitution of amount was made. The municipal officials and employees' cash advances remained unsettled despite the demand; however, existing cash advances after the service of demand, substantially reduced.
observations.reason: Some employee cash advances remained un-liquidated despite the demand.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Accountant take charge of picking up the bank statements from the depository banks and to immediately prepare the bank reconciliation statements for CY 2013 to ensure the accuracy and validity of the balances of Cash in Bank as appearing in the financial statements as of the end of CY 2013; b) Henceforth prepare the required bank reconciliation statements within the reglementary period of ten (10) days from receipt of the bank statements and to distribute the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011; the duplicate of which, together with paid checks and original copies of debit/credit memos shall be submitted to the auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: Bank reconciliation for CY 2013 had already been prepared. Make follow-up and inquiry regarding receipt of bank statement to the Treasurer Office. Some bank statements are missing with no other alternative document to substitute for the preparation of bank reconciliation. CM and DM not enclosed in the bank statement.
observations.reason: Some bank statements are missing; bank reconciliation not prepared within the reglementary period due to late submission of accounts, bank statement not directly received by the accounting office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor: a) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to transfer the book value of unserviceable properties to the Other Assets account; and b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to dispose of unserviceable properties in accordance with the disposal provisions of COA Circular 92-386 so that the municipal government can still realize additional income therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation and verification of physical inventory report was made against accounting record for proper identification of assets to be transferred and disposed. Subsidiary records for land account had been prepared based on available listing of inventory maintained in the accounting office but not supported with TCT.
observations.reason: Inventory report available is only partial and lacks details necessary for the disposal of property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: a) Observe the provisions of Section 37 of the General Appropriations Act of 2013 in the preparation of the payroll to ensure that employees’ net take home pay will not fall below P4,000.00; b) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately reconcile the cashbook with the subsidiary and general ledger and furnish the audit team the reconciliation statement with its supporting documents for verification so that the accountability of the municipal treasurer can be finally established. Henceforth, they should reconcile these two records at least quarterly as required in Section 181 I of the GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Assessor conducts general revision, with Tax program ready for implementation. Some DED are prepared and ready for implementation. The treasurer was reminded to submit his accounts. Some deductions cannot be stopped automatically due to existing MOA/contractual agreements entered by the municipality. We are effecting deductions until fully paid.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous work, transactions were not posted daily; only monthly. Some deductions cannot be stopped automatically due to existing MOA/contractual agreement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management immediately require the Municipal Engineer to hasten the preparation of the Programs of Work and Plans for the projects to be Implemented in order that the 20% Development Fund can be fully utilized and the target projects implemented in time.
observations.managementAction: Some DED are prepared. 2011 and 2012 DED are ready for implementation. Management desisted in offering sale to the public due to persistent issues regarding ownership of the lot.
observations.reason: LGU is experiencing lack of Heavy Equipment to be used and the existing equipment cannot accommodate the implementation of all the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management immediately put up tarpaulins/billboards at project sites to fully comply with the notification requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Tarpaulin visible in the project sites in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in accordance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Assessor conducts general revision, with Tax program ready for implementation. The municipal Assessor is preparing all the necessary requirements. Tax program ready for implementation.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous documents to be submitted to Register of Deeds and its difficulty in securing it, the municipality still cannot complete its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Instruct the municipal treasurer and other concerned officials to take appropriate action to have all lands belonging to the municipality be immediately titled or covered by TCT to its name duly issued by the Register of Deeds to substantiate the claim for ownership; b) Instruct the municipal treasurer and the municipal accountant to maintain their respective subsidiary records pertaining to the property accounts.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary records for land account had been prepared based on available listing of inventory maintained in the accounting office but not supported with TCT.
observations.reason: Subsidiary records prepared but not supported with TCT; waiting for issuance and difficulty in securing documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal treasurer should immediately restitute the funds of P384,325.31 initially found missing including others, if any, that may still be discovered upon final determination of her accountability. We also reiterate the recommendation that the municipal treasurer should be immediately relieved from her duties as such. The local chief executive should also institute appropriate action for her dereliction of duties and malversation of public funds.
observations.managementAction: Partial restitution of amount was made. The municipality appointed Acting Municipal Treasurer. Retired ex-Treasurer is already retiring from service. Meanwhile, the municipality appointed an Acting Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: There is still a balance due and payable to the LGU; municipality awaiting final determination; retired treasurer already retired.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately update her cashbooks and to henceforth, record her transactions in the cashbook daily pursuant to Sections 25, 26 and 27 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual, Volume II in order to facilitate the determination and verification of her accountability at any given time as well as the reconciliation of her cashbook with accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Posted transactions to cashbook every Friday. Retired ex-Treasurer mandated and required to submit her completely updated records.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous work, transactions were not posted to the cashbook on a daily basis; only monthly. Retired treasurer submitted accounts up to retirement date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to daily submit all her transaction documents to the Municipal Accountant for recording; b) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately reconcile the cashbook with the subsidiary and general ledger and furnish the audit team the reconciliation statement with its supporting documents for verification so that the accountability of the municipal treasurer can be finally established. Henceforth, they should reconcile these two records at least quarterly as required in Section 181 I of the GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The treasurer was reminded to submit his accounts. The accounting department demanded the submission of duly accomplished cash book. The treasurer submitted his accounts not daily but monthly.
observations.reason: Since the submission of accounts from the treasurer office were delayed and it is impossible to prepare immediately the reconciliation as everything will be put in place before reconciliation starts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should adopt good marketing strategies such as advertisement, public information dissemination and offering attractive incentives to the buyers of the subdivision lots. The Municipal Budget Officer should ensure that appropriations for debt servicing is equitable so that the General Fund appropriations will not be depleted.
observations.managementAction: The amount of loan had already been fully paid since 2014. Meanwhile, management desisted in offering sale to the public due to persistent issues regarding the ownership of the lot.
observations.reason: Implemented; management desisted in offering sale to the public due to ownership issues.
